seoTitle: Port Hedland vs Port Kennedy — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Port Hedland and Port Kennedy. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Port Hedland vs Port Kennedy: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Port Hedland and Port Kennedy.
heading: Port Hedland vs Port Kennedy — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Port Hedland, Western Australia and Port Kennedy, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Port Hedland has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Port Kennedy (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Port Hedland or Port Kennedy safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Port Hedland has a flood risk score of 25/100, while Port Kennedy scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Port Hedland scores 19/100 for bushfire risk. Port Kennedy scores 53/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Port Hedland or Port Kennedy?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Port Hedland has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
